indication: Worsening abdominal distension in known cirrhosis; assess ascites and portal vasculature prior to paracentesis.
technique: Grayscale and Doppler ultrasound of the abdomen.
findings: Liver is small, coarse, and nodular, consistent with cirrhosis. Splenomegaly (15 cm). LARGE-VOLUME ASCITES throughout the abdomen, with a suitable pocket in the left lower quadrant for paracentesis. Portal vein patent with hepatopetal flow. No focal hepatic mass. Gallbladder unremarkable. Kidneys normal in size.
impression: Cirrhosis with portal hypertension: splenomegaly and large-volume ascites. Patent portal vein. Adequate window for paracentesis. No focal liver lesion.
comparison: Consistent with prior known cirrhosis; ascites increased from prior visits.
